originalPrompt:
Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS

subtasks.title: Initialize project and install dependencies
subtasks.description: Create project folder, initialize npm, and install Express, Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, autoprefixer, and a dev server build tooling.
subtasks.successCriteria: Project folder contains package.json and node_modules with Express and Tailwind-related packages installed.

subtasks.title: Configure Tailwind CSS build
subtasks.description: Add Tailwind config, PostCSS config and npm scripts to build or watch Tailwind CSS output, and create base input CSS file referencing Tailwind directives.
subtasks.successCriteria: Tailwind configuration files exist and npm build/watch script produces an output CSS file containing Tailwind utilities.

subtasks.title: Implement Express server to serve frontend
subtasks.description: Create an Express app (e.g., server.js or app.js) with static file serving for built CSS and HTML, and a root route that serves the frontend.
subtasks.successCriteria: Express server starts without errors and serves index.html and the generated CSS at expected routes.

subtasks.title: Build frontend HTML with Hello World element
subtasks.description: Create index.html (or template) that includes the built Tailwind CSS, has a visible "hello world" text element, and placeholder area for the color UI controls.
subtasks.successCriteria: index.html loads in browser, displays the "hello world" text styled by Tailwind, and reserves space for UI controls.

subtasks.title: Add frontend UI to modify text color
subtasks.description: Implement JavaScript-driven UI controls (e.g., color picker, preset buttons) that change the "hello world" text color by modifying classes or inline styles live.
subtasks.successCriteria: User can change the "hello world" text color via the frontend UI and see immediate visual updates in the page.

subtasks.title: Optional: Add API to save and load color preference
subtasks.description: Add Express endpoints (POST/GET) to persist the selected color (in-memory or simple JSON file) and add frontend calls to save/load preferences.
subtasks.successCriteria: Frontend can save a selected color to the server and reload the saved color on page load (persistence verified).

subtasks.title: Test functionality and provide run instructions
subtasks.description: Manually test the app across common browsers, verify color changes and API persistence (if implemented), and write README with setup and usage steps.
subtasks.successCriteria: README with clear run/build instructions exists and tests confirm the UI changes colors and server serves files correctly.

decompositionStrategy: Top-down decomposition: set up project and tooling first, configure styling build, implement server to serve assets, build frontend structure, add interactive color UI, optionally add persistence API, then test and document.
